title: eriogonum shockleyi subsp. candidum
intro: Eriogonum shockleyi subsp. candidum, also known as Shockley's buckwheat or white-flowered buckwheat, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buckwheat family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum shockleyi subsp. candidum include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its ability to aid in the treatment of various respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
